entries.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of African history before colonialism
entries.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate an understanding of the concept of History and sources of historical information
entries.learning_activity: Describe the concept of History and the sources of historical information
teaching_learning_activities: brainstorming
group discussion
jigsaw
resourceful person
study tour
self-reflection
entries.assessment: The concept of History and sources of Historical information is clearly described
resources: On-line materials and journal articles on the concept of History and historical charts, graphs, time-line on the sources of historical information
entries.references: History Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of African history before colonialism
entries.specific_competence: 1.2 Demonstrate an understanding of theories of the origin of human beings
entries.learning_activity: (a) Describe the theories of the origin of human beings
teaching_learning_activities: Invite religious leaders and historian/archaeologist to present on the origin of human beings.
Guide students to write a report on the theories and present for discussions
entries.assessment: Theories of the origin of human beings are correctly described
resources: On-line sources, and journal articles on theories of the origin of human beings
entries.references: History Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of African history before colonialism
entries.specific_competence: 1.2 Demonstrate an understanding of theories of the origin of human beings
entries.learning_activity: (b) Describe the stages of human evolution and their technological Ages
teaching_learning_activities: Guide students in groups to role-play the physical development of a child from sitting, crawling to walking in relation to stages of human evolution from primates, homo erectus to homo-sapiens, including tools used and economic activities performed in each stage of human evolution
entries.assessment: The stages of human evolution and their technological ages are clearly described
resources: Charts, pictures, journal articles on the stages of human evolution and their technological Ages
entries.references: History Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of African history before colonialism

entries.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of African history before colonialism
entries.specific_competence: 1.3 Demonstrate an understanding of the economic, political, social and technological development of pre-colonial Africa
entries.learning_activity: (a) Describe different modes of production in pre-colonial Africa
teaching_learning_activities: Let students visit a nearby society to study ownership and characteristics of various production activities, paste each group’s findings on class walls, and let each group study other groups’ findings.
Let them relate the findings with various modes of production one after another in pre-colonial Africa
entries.assessment: Different modes of production in pre-colonial Africa are evidently described
resources: Pictures and journal articles on different modes of production in pre-colonial Africa
entries.references: History Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of African history before colonialism
entries.specific_competence: 1.3 Demonstrate an understanding of the economic, political, social and technological development of pre-colonial Africa
entries.learning_activity: (b) Explain different economic activities and their impact on pre-colonial African societies
teaching_learning_activities: Organise students to share economic activities available in the nearby society and their impacts on society. Let them relate the findings with different economic activities and their impact on pre-colonial African societies
entries.assessment: Different economic activities and their impact on pre-colonial African societies are clearly explained
resources: Journal articles and on-line sources on different economic activities and their impact on pre-colonial African societies
entries.references: History Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: 1.3 Demonstrate an understanding of the economic, political, social and technological development of pre-colonial Africa
entries.learning_activity: (c) Describe social-political organisations and education in pre-colonial African societies
